summary: Swarm-wide Claude API quota exhaustion hit at ~02:45 UTC — 29 agents rate-limited, including all studio squad members (studio-em, studio-sr, studio-qa, studio-jr, studio-designer). All studio agents are STALE (9h). This is the root cause of P0 #119 (56% failure rate). Quota resets at 11:00 UTC daily; agents should auto-recover. Two PRs need human review (#131 dynamic path fix, #133 org chart sync). Sprint goal unchanged; execution blocked until quota recovery.

p0.title: swarm reliability — 56% failure rate
p0.status: root cause identified: Claude API quota exhaustion (~02:45 UTC). 59/125 agents healthy now (47%, up from 17%). Partial recovery expected after 11:00 UTC quota reset.
p0.action: Escalate to human: increase Claude API quota tier or redistribute agent schedules to avoid morning concentration

p1.title: Agent schedules concentrate between 02:00-10:00 UTC causing daily quota exhaustion
p1.action: Spread agent cron schedules across 24h window to flatten API call curve

blockers.description: 29 agents rate-limited by Claude API daily quota at ~02:45 UTC. All studio agents (studio-em, studio-sr, studio-qa, studio-jr, studio-designer) affected. Sprint work blocked.
blockers.resolution: Increase Claude API quota tier OR spread agent cron schedules across 24h to avoid morning exhaustion

escalations: ESCALATE TO HUMAN: Claude API daily quota is exhausted before 10am UTC daily — 29/125 agents failing. This is the root cause of P0 #119. Requires quota increase or schedule redistribution. Current daily spend is compressing into a 7h window instead of spreading across 24h.

swarmHealth.checkResult: 97 agents checked, 29 skipped (log dir not present on this host). Telemetry pipeline healthy. All agents healthy per telemetry.
swarmHealth.telemetryVsLogs: DIVERGENCE: telemetry-based check shows healthy, log-based analysis (#137) shows 47/125 failing. Telemetry check measures successful events; log check measures last exit code. Quota failures don't block telemetry writes — they fail before the agent does meaningful work.

analyticsCoordination.note: analytics-pipeline failing today (quota + other failures). ETL hardening (#14) should be prioritized to handle quota-exhaustion gracefully.
